pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh lines 11-12 which creates file at $docPath\commercial_circuits.xlsx where $docPath = 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'

pi_items.metadata_value: NFPA 70 National Electrical Code, Chapter 9, Table 8
pi_items.verified_value: NFPA 70 National Electrical Code, Chapter 9, Table 8 - stranded conductor values at 75°C
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed NEC Table 8 contains conductor resistance values. Specific values in task (e.g., 14 AWG copper: 3.14 Ω/kft, 12 AWG copper: 1.98 Ω/kft, 14 AWG aluminum: 5.15 Ω/kft, 12 AWG aluminum: 3.25 Ω/kft) match NEC Table 8 stranded conductor resistance values at 75°C as stated in search results from buildmyowncabin.com and other electrical code references.
Verified in verifier.py line 145: 'expected_status = "FAIL" if expected_pct > 0.03 else "PASS"' and in task description which specifies '3.00%' as the threshold. 0.03 = 3% in decimal form.

Verified in setup_task.sh line 84: 'for ($i = 2; $i -le 51; $i++)' creates 50 data rows (rows 2-51). Also confirmed in verifier.py line 89: 'rows = list(ws.iter_rows(min_row=2, max_row=51, values_only=True))' reads exactly 50 rows.

privileged_info_summary: The NEC Voltage Drop Audit task creates a synthetic dataset of 50 electrical branch circuits in an Excel workbook at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\commercial_circuits.xlsx. The task references the NFPA 70 National Electrical Code Chapter 9, Table 8, which contains conductor resistance values in Ohms/kft for copper and aluminum stranded conductors at 75°C. The data uses verified NEC Table 8 values (e.g., 14 AWG copper: 3.14 Ω/kft, 12 AWG aluminum: 3.25 Ω/kft). The compliance limit is 0.03 (3%), used to determine whether circuits pass or fail voltage drop requirements.
